content:
Key Findings

  • Burnley Borough Council's Planning & Transportation department spent £58.4M in 2021/22 to 2025/26, accounting for 16.4% of total council expenditure.
  • The department was the second-largest spender, behind only an unnamed department with £137.5M in expenditure.
  • Environmental Services, the third-largest spending department, spent £35.7M, less than two-thirds of the Planning & Transportation budget.

Planning & Transportation: Burnley's Second-Largest Department

Burnley Borough Council's Planning & Transportation department spent £58.4M between 2021/22 and 2025/26, making it the second-largest department by expenditure. This substantial budget reflects the department's critical role in managing infrastructure, public transport, and urban planning across the borough. The department's spending represents 16.4% of the council's total expenditure of £355.2M, highlighting its significance in local governance.

Despite its importance, the department's spending is dwarfed by the largest unnamed department, which spent £137.5M, or 38.7% of the total budget. This disparity suggests that while Planning & Transportation is a key area of focus, other departments may have even greater financial demands. The third-largest spender, Environmental Services, spent £35.7M, which is less than two-thirds of the Planning & Transportation budget, underscoring the relative scale of the department's financial commitments.

Comparing Departmental Expenditure

The Planning & Transportation department's £58.4M expenditure is a significant portion of the council's overall budget. To put this into context, the next largest departments include Legal & Democratic Services at £35.3M and Regeneration & Housing at £26.5M. These figures illustrate the varying financial priorities of the council, with Planning & Transportation receiving a notably larger share of the budget compared to other key areas.

The department's spending is also notable when compared to smaller departments. For example, the Chief Executive & Corporate department spent £10.0M, less than a fifth of the Planning & Transportation budget. This comparison highlights the relative scale of the department's financial responsibilities and its importance in the council's overall operations.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Burnley Borough Council about the specific projects and initiatives funded by the Planning & Transportation department.
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about the department's plans and how public funds are being allocated.
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see which suppliers are receiving funds from the Planning & Transportation department.
  • Write to your local councillor to express your views on the department's spending priorities and how funds could be better allocated.
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain a deeper understanding of how Burnley Borough Council manages its finances.
This article is based on Burnley Borough Council's published spending data covering 2021/22 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
